specCodeDefects.specLocation: G-004-spec.md Section 2.2, line 179 JSDoc comment: '③ deleted → un-mark: delete key from statusMap only (currentMap intact)'
specCodeDefects.defect: EC-03 prose alone states 'deleted' branch → statusMap.delete(key) only. However, D5 (L201), EC-06 (L301-302), Section 2.2 branch table (L198), and Section 11.2 executable snippet (L439) all include originalMap.delete(key). Prose conflicts with four authoritative sources — likely editorial omission in EC-03.
specCodeDefects.fixApplied: applyUndo 'deleted' branch (changeMap.ts L397-409) calls both statusMap.delete(key) and originalMap.delete(key) per D5/EC-06/branch table/Section 11.2 authority (C-30 spec truth table discipline). Prevents memory leak: edited → deleted → undo sequence would leave originalMap stale entry without the delete.

feedback.coverageVerifierIndependentCheck: Verifier performed full independent Read verification of all 6 implementation files, comparing against spec Section 7 table, Section 2 API templates, Section 2.2 branch semantics, Section 11.2 executable code, and constraint requirements (C-4/C-5/C-12/C-16/C-29/C-31/C-32/C-33). Zero drift from spec values detected.
feedback.criticalFixVerification: The 'edited' branch (changeMap.ts L375-395) correctly restores from originalMap (per spec Section 2.2 branch table) not snapshotMap. This is semantically different and correct: originalMap holds the structuredClone snapshot at first edit; snapshotMap holds mount-time data that may be stale after REBUILD cycles. Verifier confirmed by direct Read of code + spec comparison.
feedback.leakFixVerification: The 'deleted' undo branch (changeMap.ts L397-409) correctly calls originalMap.delete(key) in addition to statusMap.delete(key). This prevents the originalMap leak documented in spec EC-06. Four authoritative spec sources (D5, EC-06, branch table, Section 11.2) vs one prose line — spec truth table discipline (C-30) applied correctly.
feedback.specIntegrityNote: Implementer correctly identified and documented the EC-03 prose ↔ D5/EC-06 conflict (F-06 specCodeDefects field). This is a spec quality observation (F-06 deviation handling) rather than an implementation defect. Implemented version follows authoritative sources.
feedback.cellLevelEditingDeferral: AC-004 structural/functional split correctly implemented: editedCells?: boolean in ChangeTrackingConfig + editedCellsMap stub (always new Map<string, boolean>()) in ChangeTrackingAPI. Runtime wiring deferred to G-005 per spec D4 scope-split note. Typed surface is complete and ready for G-005 integration.
feedback.allIntegrationsWired: Both new utilities fully wired: (1) applyUndo imported + called in reducer case 'UNDO'. (2) getRowStatusClassName exported in barrel, available to consumers. No dead code. C-31 functional wiring audit satisfied.
feedback.cascadingADRsComplete: ADR-007 added to decisions.md covering D7 (rowStatusStyle API), D8 (applyUndo leak fix), D9 (editedCells G-005 defer). All decision rationale documented. Ready for G-005.
